name: Kukaniloko Birthing Stones
tagline: The most sacred site on Oahu — where ali'i (royalty) were born for over 700 years, radiating mana from the island's center.
parking: Small dirt lot off Whitmore Ave — look for the state historical marker
bestTime: Early morning when you may have it to yourself — the silence adds to the gravity of the place
talkStory: For over 700 years, ali'i (royal) women traveled to this site in the geographic center of Oahu to give birth, attended by 36 chiefs who served as witnesses to validate the child's royal lineage. The petroglyphs carved into the stones are among the oldest on Oahu, and the eucalyptus-lined path you walk today follows what was once a ceremonial approach. The stones are believed to hold immense mana — do not sit on or touch them, and do not leave offerings unless guided by a cultural practitioner. Most locals consider this the single most sacred place on the island; treat it accordingly.
